sections.paragraph:
ἐκφωνέω (s. φωνέω, φωνή; Dionys. Hal., Comp. Verb. 14 et al.; Just., D. 97, 1?; Tat., Ath.; magic pap: PWarr 21, 32) 1 aor. ἐξεφώνησα; pf. ἐκπεφώνηκα (Tat., Ath.); pf. pass. 3 sg. ἐκπεφώνηται (Ath. 24, 5); plpf. 3 sg. ἐκπεφώνητο (Just., D. 97, 1 as corrected in cod. Paris) cry out (so Plut., Caes. 739 [66, 8]; Philo, De Jos. 51) Lk 16:24 v.l.—DELG s.v. φωνή.

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PWarr PWarr = The Warren Papyri—List 4

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 312
